Title: Dark Mode Toggle — Watermelon theme activates dark variant on toggle
Preconditions: Storefront accessible; Watermelon theme preset configured for store or org; user signed in
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] homepage fully loaded with Watermelon light theme visible
[ACT] locate theme/dark mode toggle control in header or footer
[ACT] click dark mode toggle to enable dark mode
[WAIT] page re-renders with dark color palette applied
Assertions:
[DOM] page background changes from light (#ffffff region) to dark (#040303)
[DOM] body text color updates to light (#ebe5e5)
[DOM] header background updates to dark (#141010 top / #332829 bottom)
[DOM] dark mode toggle shows active/enabled state
[STATE] dark mode state is active (not reverted after 2s)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors or CSS variable warnings during toggle
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx requests triggered by toggle action
Failure_Signals: Toggle unresponsive after click; body background remains white after 2s; JS error on toggle; no visual color change

Title: Dark Mode — OS Preference Respected (prefers-color-scheme: dark)
Preconditions: Watermelon theme active for store; browser/OS set to dark color scheme preference
Assertions:
[DOM] dark mode applied automatically without manual toggle click
[DOM] body background is dark (#040303)
[STATE] dark mode toggle (if present) reflects the auto-detected dark state
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] no errors during auto dark mode detection
Failure_Signals: Page ignores OS preference and loads light theme; CSS media query not applied; toggle shows wrong state

Title: Dark Mode Persistence — Dark state survives page refresh
Preconditions: Watermelon dark mode enabled via toggle; user on homepage
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] enable dark mode toggle
[WAIT] dark theme applied
[KEY] F5 (or browser refresh)
[WAIT] page fully reloaded
Assertions:
[DOM] dark mode remains active after refresh (body bg #040303)
[STATE] dark mode toggle still shows enabled state
[DOM] no flash of light theme before dark mode re-applies
Failure_Signals: Dark mode resets to light after refresh; FOUC of light theme before dark settles; toggle shows disabled after refresh

Title: Dark Mode Persistence — Dark state survives navigation across pages
Preconditions: Watermelon dark mode enabled; user on homepage
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] enable dark mode toggle
[WAIT] dark theme applied
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog (or any category page)
[WAIT] catalog page loaded
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/search?q=test
[WAIT] search page loaded
Assertions:
[STATE] dark mode remains active on catalog page
[STATE] dark mode remains active on search results page
[DOM] body bg is consistently #040303 across all page navigations
Failure_Signals: Dark mode drops on SPA route change; light flash between pages

Title: Dark Mode Persistence — Deep link opens in dark mode
Preconditions: User has previously enabled dark mode (persisted in localStorage or cookie)
Steps:
[ACT] ensure dark mode preference is stored (enable toggle, close tab)
[NAV] open a new tab directly to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog (deep link, not homepage)
[WAIT] catalog page fully loaded
Assertions:
[DOM] dark mode applied immediately on deep-linked page (#040303 background)
[STATE] no light-mode flash before dark mode loads
[DOM] dark mode toggle shows correct enabled state
Failure_Signals: Deep link opens in light mode ignoring stored preference; FOUC of light before dark

Title: WCAG AA — Primary CTA button (#e73433) contrast ratio audit
Preconditions: Watermelon dark mode active; browser DevTools or axe/accessibility tool available; a CTA button with accent red background visible
Steps:
[NAV] navigate to a page with accent-red CTA button in dark mode
[ACT] enable dark mode toggle
[WAIT] dark theme applied
[ACT] open DevTools > Elements; select the CTA button element
[ACT] use DevTools Computed styles or Accessibility panel to measure contrast ratio of white text on #e73433 background
[ACT] alternatively run axe DevTools extension on the element
Assertions:
[FORMAT] contrast ratio of white (#ffffff) on #e73433 measured and recorded
[STATE] contrast ratio is >= 4.5:1 for normal text (WCAG AA) OR >= 3:1 for large text (18pt+)
[DOM] button is annotated as failing contrast if ratio is 4.25:1 (below 4.5:1 threshold)
Failure_Signals: Contrast ratio below 4.5:1 for normal-sized button text — known BUG-02 candidate; ratio 4.25:1 measured in analysis
Cleanup: Document measured ratio; report as accessibility bug if < 4.5:1 (suspected 4.25:1 per prior analysis)

Title: WCAG AA — Status color (-500 variants) contrast on dark card surfaces
Preconditions: Watermelon dark mode active; page with status badges or labels visible (e.g. order status, stock status)
Steps:
[NAV] navigate to a page with status indicators (orders list, PDP stock status, cart notifications)
[ACT] enable dark mode toggle
[WAIT] dark theme applied
[ACT] identify status-colored elements (success, warning, error, info badges)
[ACT] inspect computed background/text colors for each status variant in DevTools
[ACT] measure or estimate contrast ratio using Accessibility panel
Assertions:
[FORMAT] each status -500 color (success, warning, danger, info) contrast ratio recorded
[STATE] each status color pair meets >= 4.5:1 ratio for text, OR >= 3:1 for large text
[DOM] status labels are legible in dark mode
Failure_Signals: Status -500 colors too dark relative to dark card surface — insufficient contrast; BUG-03 candidate
Cleanup: Document ratio per status color; report as accessibility bug if failing

Title: WCAG AA — neutral-300 border contrast on dark surfaces
Preconditions: Watermelon dark mode active; page with bordered UI components (cards, inputs, separators) visible
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] enable dark mode toggle
[WAIT] dark theme applied
[ACT] locate UI elements with visible borders (form inputs, product cards, dividers)
[ACT] inspect computed border-color in DevTools
[ACT] check border color is the neutral-300 token value
[ACT] evaluate contrast of the border color against adjacent background
Assertions:
[FORMAT] neutral-300 border color value recorded (expected: a mid-gray)
[STATE] border contrast ratio against adjacent background is >= 3:1 (WCAG AA for non-text contrast)
[DOM] borders are visually distinguishable from card/page background
Failure_Signals: Border invisible on dark surface (borderline contrast per BUG-04 analysis); inputs look borderless
Cleanup: Document measured ratio; report as bug if border contrast < 3:1

Title: WCAG — Full page axe accessibility scan in Watermelon dark mode
Preconditions: Watermelon dark mode active; axe DevTools or Lighthouse available
Steps:

[ACT] enable dark mode toggle
[WAIT] dark theme fully applied and page stable

[WAIT] audit completes
Assertions:
[STATE] no new accessibility violations introduced by dark mode tokens beyond the 3 known candidates (BUG-02, BUG-03, BUG-04)
[STATE] critical accessibility violations count is 0 (excluding known documented issues)
[DOM] all focusable elements have visible focus indicators in dark mode
Failure_Signals: New critical axe violations beyond known 3; focus indicators invisible in dark mode; missing ARIA labels
Cleanup: Document all violations; create bug tickets for any not in BUG-02/03/04

Title: Theme Switch — Light to dark toggle (watermelon light → watermelon dark)

[FORMAT] header colors update to dark variants

Title: Theme Switch — Switching from Watermelon dark back to Watermelon light via admin preset change
Preconditions: Admin access to store or org White Labeling settings; Watermelon dark active; dark mode toggle NOT in use (using preset-based switch)
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}
[ACT] sign in as admin
[BLADE] open Stores > select store > White Labeling blade
[ACT] change themePresetName from 'watermelon.dark' or equivalent to 'watermelon' (light)
[SAVE] save White Labeling settings
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] storefront refreshed with new theme
Assertions:
[FORM] White Labeling blade shows updated preset name after save
[TOAST] success notification in admin
[FORMAT] storefront body background is now Watermelon light (not dark #040303)
[STATE] no dark mode colors visible when using light preset
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no admin blade JS errors
[NETWORK] GET whiteLabelingSettings returns updated themePresetName
Failure_Signals: Admin save fails; dark colors remain on storefront after preset change; success toast not shown
Cleanup: restore original preset in White Labeling

Title: White Labeling Integration — GraphQL whiteLabelingSettings returns watermelon dark preset name
Preconditions: Watermelon dark preset configured for org; GraphiQL accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql; Bearer token available
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[AUTH] set Authorization: Bearer <token> in Headers panel
[ACT] click query editor, Ctrl+A, Backspace to clear
[ACT] type query: { whiteLabelingSettings(organizationId: "<org-id>" storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}") { themePresetName } }
[GQL] execute query (Ctrl+Enter or click Run button)
[READ] read response panel
Assertions:
[ERRORS] errors[] is empty in response
[DATA] data.whiteLabelingSettings.themePresetName matches the Watermelon dark preset name configured in admin
[DATA] value is non-null
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; themePresetName null or returns wrong preset name (e.g. 'default')
